title: Ontology/codelist + NC/custom-source acceptance policy
context: Coded fields in cancer datasets map to controlled vocabularies (ICD-O-3, NCIt, HGVS, MONDO, SNOMED CT) and some sources carry non-commercial/custom licenses (COSMIC, OncoKB, GENIE, IARC). Binding guardrail: open-access / aggregate / de-identified data only; controlled-access and identifiable data are EXCLUDED at gate-002; provenance on every assertion; the deliverable is documentation about the data, never the data itself. This policy decides which ontologies and source licenses may be used and how restricted vocabularies are handled.
objective: Define the ontology/codelist acceptance policy and the non-commercial/custom-source acceptance rules that gate and crosswalk tooling must enforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Names the accepted ontologies/codelists and pinned-version policy (ICD-O-3, NCIt, HGVS, MONDO) and the rule for restricted vocabularies (e.g. SNOMED CT): link-out by code only, never republish licensed term text.
Defines the NC/custom-source decision for COSMIC, OncoKB, GENIE, IARC with rationale, consistent with gate-002 license rules (no default-allow; missing/unparseable license = FLAG/EXCLUDE).
States that every coded-value meaning carries a provenance.sourceRef and that unsourced or unlicensed term text is invalid.
Policy is committed as a document and is referenced by gate-002 and the crosswalk tooling as the authority for ontology/license decisions.
